skill.name: create-svg-from-prompt
skill.description: Creates SVG artwork from a text prompt by generating an image with Gemini and tracing it with autotrace.
skill.summary: Generate traced SVG images from text prompts through Gemini and autotrace.
skill.author: 0x6a77

security_audit.summary: The static findings for privileged shell commands, network access, and environment key handling are true positives. The weak cryptography findings appear to be false positives caused by base64 decoding, but the skill still presents high risk because it asks agents to install software with sudo, clone and build external source, and send prompts plus an API key to a third-party API.

high_findings.title: Privileged Source Build And Install
high_findings.description: The setup instructions run apt commands with sudo, clone autotrace from GitHub, build it locally, and run sudo make install. This can modify the host system and execute unreviewed build scripts with elevated privileges.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The shell commands and sudo usage are explicit in the setup block. This is a direct operational risk even though it is not proof of malicious intent.

high_findings.title: External API Call With Environment API Key
high_findings.description: The workflow sends a generated image prompt to a Google Gemini endpoint using a Gemini API key from the environment. This exposes user prompt content to a third party and requires secret handling in an inline shell command.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The curl command, API endpoint, API key header, and environment variable source are explicit. The risk is clear, although using Gemini may be functionally intended.

high_findings.title: Prompt-Derived Shell Substitution Risk
high_findings.description: The workflow places the user-derived image prompt and output file name into a long shell command. Without explicit quoting or validation rules, crafted prompt text or filenames could break command structure.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command includes placeholders populated from user request content. The file does not provide escaping, JSON construction, or filename validation safeguards.

medium_findings.title: Remote Response Piped Into Local Tooling
medium_findings.description: The command decodes data from the API response and pipes it directly into autotrace. Malformed or unexpected image data could trigger parser bugs or resource exhaustion in local tooling.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The pipeline from network response to base64 decoding and autotrace is explicit. The impact depends on autotrace behavior and sandboxing, so confidence is high but not absolute.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The static weak cryptography findings appear related to base64 decoding and the Gemini model string, not to cryptographic hashing or encryption. No evidence found of a weak cryptographic algorithm being used for security.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The visible file only shows base64 decoding for image data extraction. I did not find security-sensitive cryptographic operations in the skill file.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backtick Execution Findings Are Partly False Positives
low_findings.description: Some Ruby or shell backtick findings point to Markdown inline code and fenced code formatting. The fenced shell commands are still real operational instructions, but the Markdown syntax itself is not Ruby command execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file is Markdown, not Ruby. The backticks mark code formatting, although the displayed commands remain relevant security evidence.

dangerous_patterns.title: Sudo Package Installation
dangerous_patterns.description: The setup block instructs the agent to update packages, install dependencies, and run make install with sudo. This grants broad host modification capability.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The sudo commands are explicit and require elevated privileges. Their impact is inherent to the commands shown.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell Pipeline Combining Network, Secret, And Local Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: A single shell pipeline combines curl, an API key header, text extraction, base64 decoding, and autotrace processing. This creates a broad failure surface for secret exposure and command misuse.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The combined command is directly visible in the workflow. It matches the static dangerous-combination heuristic with clear semantic support.

content.user_title: Create SVG Art From Text Prompts
content.value_statement: Teams often need quick vector artwork without manual tracing. This skill describes a workflow that generates an image from a prompt and converts it into SVG form.
actual_capabilities: Guides creation of SVG artwork from a user text prompt.
Uses Gemini image generation as the raster image source.
Uses autotrace to convert image output into SVG format.
Suggests prompt rewriting from SVG language to image language.
Defines an output filename based on the requested image.
limitations: Requires autotrace and several native build dependencies.
Requires a Gemini API key available in the environment.
Sends user prompt content to an external Google API.
Generated SVG quality depends on the raster image and tracing result.

use_cases.title: Prototype Interface Icons
use_cases.description: A product designer can request simple SVG concepts for early interface mockups.

use_cases.title: Create Draft Marketing Graphics
use_cases.description: A marketer can generate rough vector illustrations for campaign drafts and creative exploration.

use_cases.title: Prepare Editable Vector Assets
use_cases.description: A developer can create a starting SVG asset for later cleanup in design tools.

prompt_templates.title: Basic SVG Request
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an SVG of a simple blue rocket launching upward.
prompt_templates.scenario: Use this for a straightforward icon or illustration request.

prompt_templates.title: Style And Subject
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an SVG of a flat-style coffee cup with steam, using warm colors and clean shapes.
prompt_templates.scenario: Use this when the subject and visual style both matter.

prompt_templates.title: Brand Asset Draft
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an SVG mascot concept for a project management app, with friendly geometric shapes and no text.
prompt_templates.scenario: Use this to explore brand visuals before manual refinement.

prompt_templates.title: Detailed Scene Conversion
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an SVG scene of a compact city garden at sunrise, with simplified buildings, plants, and a clear foreground.
prompt_templates.scenario: Use this for more complex compositions that still need clean vector output.

output_examples.input: Create an SVG of a minimal mountain badge.
output_examples.output: A traced SVG file representing a simple badge with mountains, sky, and clean outer shape.

output_examples.input: Create an SVG of a friendly robot assistant.
output_examples.output: An SVG illustration draft with a robot character, simplified features, and editable vector paths.

output_examples.input: Create an SVG of a small delivery van icon.
output_examples.output: A vector-style vehicle asset suitable for review and manual cleanup in a design editor.

best_practices: Use short prompts with clear subjects, style, and composition.
Review generated SVG files before using them in production.
Run network and tracing commands in a sandboxed environment.
anti_patterns: Do not provide secrets directly in the prompt text.
Do not run privileged setup commands on sensitive hosts.
Do not expect the traced SVG to be clean production artwork without review.

faq.question: Does this skill create SVG files directly?
faq.answer: It describes a workflow that generates an image first, then traces that image into SVG format.

faq.question: Does it require an external service?
faq.answer: Yes. The workflow calls the Google Gemini image generation API.

faq.question: Does it need an API key?
faq.answer: Yes. It expects a Gemini API key from the GEMINI_API_KEY environment variable.

faq.question: Is the output ready for production use?
faq.answer: Usually not. Traced SVG output should be inspected and cleaned before production use.

faq.question: Can it work without installing autotrace?
faq.answer: No. The documented workflow depends on autotrace for vector conversion.

faq.question: What is the main security concern?
faq.answer: The skill combines privileged setup commands, network access, and API key handling in shell workflows.
